stakes.what_this_is: A 103-line Python walker (verify.py) that runs over skills/dashboard/ and exits 0 iff every entry-point file (render.py, snapshot.py, narrate.py, html.py) is present, parses, imports nothing from the LLM SDK or nondeterminism denylists, and declares the constants + functions a Foundation-2 collector requires. In one line: it moves the dashboard skill's '1.0 deterministic, no LLM in renders' claim from prose to a thing that gets checked.
stakes.why_we_build_it: CLAUDE.md states the load-bearing rule: 'If a 3.0-under-0.4 cannot walk it, it is not 4.0.' The dashboard skill is currently sub-4.0 by the experiment's own rule because no walker confirms its shape. The gap was auto-detected by skills/gap_audit/collectors/skill_without_verifier.py — a 1.0 program noticing absence — not a hand-flagged item. Foundation discipline says detected absences get closed.
if_approved: skills/dashboard/verify.py lands (103 lines, deterministic, no LLM, no nondeterminism). Any agent or human can run it.
The dashboard you are reading this on becomes walkable — meta: it graduates to a 4.0 candidate.
Gap data point skill_without_verifier:21d380eb... flips from live to resolved on the next gap_audit run.
Floor ratio (1.0+2.0)/3.0 inches up by one new 1.0 file.

Precedent: structural verifiers (presence + parse + import-deny) are an acceptable closure shape for skills claiming '1.0 deterministic, no SDK.' The paired proposal (verifier-for-regime-audit-report) below should follow this decision.
if_rejected: skill_without_verifier:21d380eb... stays open in the gap_audit dataset.
Dashboard remains sub-4.0 until something else closes the gap (a different verifier shape, or a rewrite of the skill's claim).
Your reason becomes a policy signal — e.g. 'structural-only verifiers are not enough.' Consistency means the paired proposal below should also be rejected for the same reason.
Strengthens the case for the parameterized-bundle-verifier draft as the eventual replacement shape.

connects_to.label: Foundation rule that motivates this proposal
Paired pending proposal — same shape, different target (decide together)
Already-promoted proposal of same shape — precedent
Already-rejected proposal of same shape (target had a behavioral verifier — wrong-fit there)
